origin.session_summary: Applied the F1-F6 logic-audit patches to the (temporarily unlocked) engine and re-locked it; adopted the 2026-07-18 dataset; built scripts/ingest.sh with a drift gate; created the docs/TODO.md ledger; scaffolded and built website v1 (Vue 3 + TS + Tailwind v4); pushed the repo public and deployed the dashboard live to GitHub Pages.

do_not_redo.item: F1-F6 are APPLIED and committed (engine tests repointed to the frozen fixture, F2-F4 core patches output-neutral by content-hash proof, F5/F6 documented in AUDIT_BRIEF). Do not re-apply. F6 (overnight split drops one minute) is documented behaviour - never "fix" it unasked.

Dataset decision: data/exports/hours_export_2026-07-18.csv is authoritative (user-confirmed). The 06-25 export disagrees with later data on 19 June dates and is kept ONLY as provenance - never re-adopt it.
Section 7 hosting/identity: DECIDED public repo + public Pages, identity-light JSON (no subject). Exposure trade-offs were explained and accepted 2026-07-19. Recorded in WEBSITE_PLAN section 7.

Front-end principle: option a+c decided. sumMinutes in website/src/lib/format.ts is the sanctioned browser aggregation (CLAUDE.md amended); above_contract_minutes moves into the engine at schema 1.2.0, then sumMinutes is deleted.
Git transport: SSH (works in-container, tested). Remote origin = git@github.com:filipejunqueira/nhs-log-hours-db-manager.git.
Pages source is set to "GitHub Actions" (user flipped it after the auto-enablement chose legacy Jekyll). Actions bumped to node24-era majors; the Node 20 deprecation warning is resolved.
Audit docs consolidated: docs/logic-audit_2026-07-06.md is the REPORT, docs/logic-audit-prompt_2026-07-06.md is the commissioning prompt. They were never duplicates.

decision.what: scripts/ingest.sh compares new exports against the CANONICAL CSV (engine_v2/data/filipe_working_hours_log.csv), not the newest-named export.
decision.why: Legacy exports are named by export date and outsort entry-date names, so an accepted correction would re-flag forever; the canonical CSV is by definition the last accepted state.

decision.what: Engine determines every hours figure; browser may only re-present (divide by 60, HH:MM, labels, sumMinutes of banded values), all confined to website/src/lib/format.ts.
decision.why: Audience is a non-technical payroll reader - presentation clarity supersedes strict zero-calculation; the red line is that nothing pay-determining is computed outside the testable Python engine.

decision.what: Non-config documentation lives in docs/; docs/TODO.md is the project ledger (Now/Next/Later/Done), synced every wrap-up.
decision.why: User preference; fixes the tracking gap that made this session's restore detective work.

current_state.item: Branch main == origin/main at 3dc8c89; working tree CLEAN; branch logic-audit-2026-07-06 also pushed.
Site LIVE and verified: https://filipejunqueira.github.io/nhs-log-hours-db-manager/ serves the Vue app; its web_data.json returns 16808 min, schema 1.1.0.
Test suites at last full run this session: engine 67/67 (rules 11, core 39, emit 17), characterisation 29/29.
Website builds clean: npx vue-tsc --noEmit and npm run build (run from website/ - vue-tsc is a local devDependency).
plan.md is marked EXECUTED; the next slice needs a fresh plan-gate.

next_steps.step: See docs/TODO.md - it is current as of this session's end (Done log includes the live deploy). Now item 1: v1.1 components (cross-tab, cumulative chart, stats panel - data already in the JSON).

TODO.md Now item 2: plan the user-requested improvements (per-month breakdown, unpaid-hours tracking) - both point at engine schema 1.2.0; monthly banding across pay-week boundaries needs a definition decision first; unpaid-hours needs a paid-up-to input whose home (config file vs money.py) is undecided.
TODO.md Now item 3: scripts/update.sh automation chain (BUILD_NOTES section 5 caveats).

files_touched.file: F2-F4 patches (only engine code change; lock now restored)
repointed + 4 defect pins flipped to fixed-behaviour pins
CSV intake with drift gate (sandbox-tested, 8 scenarios)
full Vue 3 + TS + Tailwind v4 app, v1 complete

files_to_read.file: executed plan for context; write a fresh one for the next slice
sections 4-5 for v1.1 components; docs/BUILD_NOTES.md corrections still win

domain_state.engine: Schema 1.1.0, locked (deny rules active). Pure stdlib. Suites: 67 engine checks + 29 characterisation, all green. Next lift = 1.2.0: above_contract_minutes, monthly array, possibly Hours-column cross-check and plausibility warnings (see TODO Later).
domain_state.website: v1 live on GitHub Pages via .github/workflows/deploy.yml (builds website/ on Node 24, deploys dist/ on push to main). vite base = /nhs-log-hours-db-manager/. Data flow: engine_v2/web_data.json is copied manually to website/public/web_data.json (update.sh will automate).

warnings.warning: The PostToolUse ruff hook REFORMATS ENTIRE .py FILES on Write/Edit. For surgical diffs in audited code (engine_v2), apply patches via Bash-scripted uniqueness-checked replacements, never the Edit tool; this bit us once and the files had to be restored from HEAD.
cp/rm are aliased interactive in this container: use `command cp -f`, `rm -f`, and redirect stdin from /dev/null in scripts.
Bash tool cwd persists between calls - running npx vue-tsc from the repo root instead of website/ downloads a broken global copy and fails with ERR_PACKAGE_PATH_NOT_EXPORTED (red herring).

data/exports/ mixes two naming conventions: the two legacy files are named by EXPORT date, new ingests by LATEST-ENTRY date. Safe because the drift baseline is the canonical CSV, but do not "clean up" the legacy names - they are provenance.

Engine work for 1.2.0 requires: lift deny rules deliberately (backup settings.json first), every changed byte traces to the change spec, characterisation suite pins updated deliberately with old/new reported, and output-neutrality checked for figures that should not move. Copy this session's discipline (see docs/TODO.md Done log and git history around 76ad636..cbf9543).
